title: Reconcile `exportVerilog` and `exportSplitVerilog` behavior.
body:
Some previous discussion here: https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/2260

There are two behaviors for `exportVerilog` that are currently not specified by lowering options:
- Whether or not to separate each top-level module into its own Verilog file
- Whether or not Verilog is emitted to a stream (potentially with a comment indicating where file splits occur if file splits are explicit, i.e. using `#hw.outputFile`), or written directly to the file system

Currently we only have two combinations of these options:
**exportVerilog** - do not separate, output to stream
**exportSplitVerilog** - separate, write to filesystem

For testing, it may be useful to have a mode which separates modules, but does not write to the filesystem. Even better would be if there was no difference in behavior between the two (i.e. modules would always be "separated").

Unfortunately, when using the `separateModules` the following tests fail because separating modules actually moves some of the resulting Verilog around:
```
Conversion/ExportVerilog/sv-dialect.mlir
Conversion/ExportVerilog/verilog-basic.mlir
Dialect/FIRRTL/SFCTests/GrandCentralInterfaces/Wire.fir
Dialect/MSFT/location.mlir
```

I'm not sure what the correct thing to do here is, maybe someone else can take a look.
